analysis_results.content: 4-panel structural view of designed Kemp eliminase active sites. Panel A: Overall enzyme fold, with active site highlighted: pink surface = transition state/substrate, cyan surface = catalytic residues. Panel B: Initial design active site residues: W275, Q90, E237, A21, H83, T44, T265, D127, Q42, R81. Panel C: Optimized designed active site with bound transition state (TS, gold): mutated residues include A21→M21, Q90→Y90, with E237 forming a hydrogen bond to the TS, remaining residues conserved from initial design. Panel D: Alternative designed variant active site: mutated residues include T44→W44, H83→G83, E237→M237, T265→S265, Q42→M42, R81→G81, with Q90 and W275 conserved, bound TS (gold) visible.

analysis_results.figure_id: Table 1: Kinetic Parameters of Designed Enzymes
analysis_results.content: Table reporting kinetic parameters for designed Kemp eliminase variants across 3 protein scaffolds (1GOR, 1A53, 1THF). Key findings: 1) The highest activity variant HG-3 (S265T mutation on HG-2 backbone) has k_cat = 0.68 ± 0.04 s⁻¹, K_m = 1.6 ± 0.1 mM, k_cat/K_m = 425.0 ± 36.5 s⁻¹ M⁻¹, and rate acceleration k_cat/k_uncat = 5.9 × 10⁵. 2) Parental HG-2 has k_cat/K_m = 123.2 ± 0.8 s⁻¹ M⁻¹, while HG-2 mutations reduce activity: D127N (1.6 ± 0.2 s⁻¹ M⁻¹), S265A (41.0 ± 1.1 s⁻¹ M⁻¹), K50A (37.7 ± 0.4 s⁻¹ M⁻¹), G81A (17.2 ± 0.5 s⁻¹ M⁻¹). 3) 1A53-2 has k_cat = 0.09 ± 0.01 s⁻¹, K_m = 1.4 ± 0.2 mM, k_cat/K_m = 64.2 ± 11.6 s⁻¹ M⁻¹, rate acceleration = 7.8 × 10⁴. 4) MD prediction of activity matches experimental measurements for all variants with reported MD data.

analysis_results.figure_id: Figure 2: Catalytic Mechanism and Active Site Comparisons
analysis_results.content: 6-panel structural/mechanistic figure. Panel A: Proposed Kemp elimination catalytic mechanism: general base (Asp/Glu) deprotonates substrate, H-bond donor (Ser/Thr/Tyr) stabilizes transition state, π-stacking residue (Phe/Trp) interacts with the aromatic substrate ring. Panel B: Overlay of designed active site (yellow) and native Kemp eliminase active site (green) with bound TS model, key residues: D239, H209, E237, R276, W275, Y90. Panels C-F: Active site ligand binding poses: Panel C: TS model (green) bound to designed active site with residues W44, D127, S265, K50, hydrogen bonds marked with dashed lines. Panels D-F: 5-NBT substrate analog (yellow) bound to HG-3 variant active site, showing conserved hydrogen bonding interactions between 5-NBT, D127, and S265.

analysis_results.figure_id: Figure 3: MD Simulations of Active Site Geometry
analysis_results.content: 4-panel molecular dynamics simulation results. Panel A: 12 ns time trace of distance (Å) between 5-NBZ substrate proton and Asp127 Oδ for initial design: stable ~3 Å distance for first 6 ns, then jumps to ~7 Å (unproductive binding pose). Panel B: 20 ns time trace of same distance for optimized HG-3 variant: distance remains ~2.5 Å for full simulation, indicating stable productive binding. Panel C: 2D histogram of angle θ (5-NBZ C-H - - Oδ-Asp127, degrees) vs distance d (Å) for initial design: broad cluster at d ~2.5 Å, θ ~90-160°. Panel D: 2D histogram of same parameters for HG-3 variant: tight cluster at d ~2.5 Å, θ ~140-170°, showing significantly more optimal geometry for deprotonation by the Asp127 general base. Axes: Panels A/B: x-axis = simulation time (ns), y-axis = distance (Å); Panels C/D: x-axis = distance d (Å), y-axis = angle θ (degrees).

Reaction scheme for the Kemp elimination of 5-nitrobenzisoxazole: Step 1: General base (:B) deprotonates the C3 position of 5-nitrobenzisoxazole, N-O bond cleavage initiates in the substrate. Step 2: Formation of the charged transition state (‡) with partial B-H bond formation and partial double bond formation in the substrate. Step 3: Reaction completes to form 2-cyano-4-nitrophenolate product.

2-panel structural overlay. Panel A: Overlay of designed transition state model (green) and 5-NBT substrate analog (yellow) bound to the active site: π-stacking interactions with W210 (upper) and W110 (lower) are conserved, E178 forms a hydrogen bond to the TS/substrate. Panel B: 5-NBT (yellow) bound to the active site, showing π-stacking with W210 and W110, E178 positioned to act as general base for proton abstraction from the substrate.

Initial velocity (v₀, M/sec) vs substrate concentration ([5-NBZ], M) plot for 4 HG-series variants: 1) HG-3 (HG-2/S265T, black circles solid line): highest activity, saturating at ~1.25 ×10⁻⁶ M/sec at 1 mM 5-NBZ. 2) HG-2 (black squares dashed line): moderate activity, ~6 ×10⁻⁷ M/sec at 1 mM substrate. 3) HG-2/S265A (black diamonds dotted line): low activity, ~2 ×10⁻⁷ M/sec at 1 mM substrate. 4) HG-2/D127N (black triangles dotted line): negligible near-zero activity across all substrate concentrations. X-axis range: 0 to 1×10⁻³ M, y-axis range: 0 to 1.5×10⁻⁶ M/sec, error bars show standard deviation of replicate measurements.

## Enzyme Extraction Summary: Kemp Eliminase Dataset (Privett 2012)
### Dataset Overview
This analysis covers 12 unique Kemp eliminase variants across three protein scaffolds (1GOR, 1A53, 1THF), all catalyzing the Kemp elimination of 5-nitrobenzisoxazole to 2-cyano-4-nitrophenolate.

### Significant Improvements
1. **Top variant HG-3** (single S265T mutation from HG-2 parent) has the highest measured catalytic efficiency of 425.0 M⁻¹s⁻¹, representing a 3.4-fold improvement over its parent HG-2 (123.2 M⁻¹s⁻¹) and 265.6-fold improvement over the catalytic base knockout control HG-2 D127N (1.6 M⁻¹s⁻¹).
2. HG-2 is 1.9x more efficient than the top 1A53 scaffold variant (1A53-2, 64.2 M⁻¹s⁻¹).
3. Three variants (1A53-1, 1THF-1, HG-1) show no detectable catalytic activity.

### Data Gaps & Quality Notes
- Kinetic parameter coverage: 16.7% for kcat, 16.7% for Km, 75.0% for kcat/Km, 0% for Tm.
- 9 variants have reported mutations that conflict with canonical scaffold residue sequences.
- Only 2 variants have full Michaelis-Menten kinetic parameters (kcat and Km) reported, as substrate saturation could not be achieved for remaining active variants.
